description:
Advances: specled.use_tier

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Realization.Use.consumers_for/2 enumerates consumer modules via Compiler.Tracer imported_macro edges (recomputed per-check, NOT persisted); use-tier hash composes provider expanded_behavior hash + sorted consumer module list; macro-provider root-cause finding format (one finding per provider drift with root_cause.provider, tier: :use, hash_prefix_before/after as 8-char hex, consumers_affected count, consumers list); cyclic-deps tiebreak by lexicographic subject id (extend Drift.dedupe/2 + tests); Scenario 4 integration test (scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs); extended test/specled_ex/realization/drift_test.exs with cyclic fixture.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/use_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/drift_test.exs (extended)
  - mix test test/integration/scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs --include integration
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.use_tier.scenario.consumers_current_not_persisted
  - specled.use_tier.scenario.provider_drift_one_finding (Scenario 4)
  - specled.api_boundary.scenario.cyclic_dedupe_tiebreak_stable (Drift extended tests)

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (extend existing; respect 150 LOC cap)
- test/specled_ex/realization/use_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/drift_test.exs (extend)
- test/integration/scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex (S5a) — consume but do not modify
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (S3b)
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/** (consume tracer output only)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT persist consumer lists in state.json (scope cut #27); always recompute.
- Do NOT emit per-consumer drift findings when a root_cause covers them.
- 8-char hash prefixes required in root_cause output — shorter prefixes collide across history, longer are illegible.
- Drift.dedupe/2 still exports exactly one function; no new public API.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Drift.dedupe/2 public surface unchanged (still one exported function)
- [ ] Drift module <= 150 LOC including moduledoc
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

  - lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (extended, 105 -> 135 LOC, single public fn preserved)
  - test/specled_ex/realization/use_test.exs (NEW, 10 tests)
  - test/specled_ex/realization/drift_test.exs (extended, +4 tests on macro-provider dedup)
  - test/integration/scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs (NEW, scenario 4 gate)
  - .spec/specs/use_tier.spec.md (status draft -> active; 3 execute: false -> true on own subject stubs)
  - .spec/state.json (regen: 3 surface_target_missing warnings cleared, strength upgraded)

Tests added: 15 new tests
  - SpecLedEx.Realization.UseTest: consumers_for/2 (4 tests), scenario.consumers_current_not_persisted (1), hash/2 composition (5)
  - SpecLedEx.Realization.DriftTest: macro-provider root_cause dedupe (4 tests)
  - SpecLedEx.Integration.ScenarioMacroProviderDriftTest: scenario 4 gate (1)

Verification stubs flipped:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/use_test.exs (covers enumerate_consumers, provider_hash_composes)
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/drift_test.exs (covers root_cause_dedupe, hash_prefix_length)
  - mix test test/integration/scenario_macro_provider_drift_test.exs --include integration (covers scenario_macro_provider_drift)

Local sanity:
  - mix test --include integration: 389 tests, 0 failures (was 374 pre-S5b)
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix spec.check: 0 use_tier findings; 11 pre-existing branch findings (verified baseline by stashing S5b changes — same 11 on HEAD e456fd2). Same noise S5a-S5a iter2 carried through.

Summary: Built 5th realization tier (Use) consuming the tracer ETF without persisting consumer lists, composed-hash sensitive to provider-expansion change OR consumer set change. Extended Drift.dedupe to recognize tier: :use deltas as forced roots with a synthesized root_cause map (provider MFA, 8-char hash prefixes, consumers_affected, consumers). Drift module stays single-function and under 150 LOC. Subject specled.use_tier flipped to active — all five must requirements covered.

title: S5b: use tier + macro root-cause dedup
